rates: on-hold — fed funds 3.50-3.75%; hold ~97% priced for Jun 17
inflation: elevated — May CPI 4.2% YoY (released Jun 10); energy-driven

note: Market is well above its 200-day average (+9.6%). Valuations are elevated on both trailing and Shiller-CAPE measures. Inflation remains hot at 4.2% YoY, keeping the Fed on hold. The FOMC meeting (decision Wed) is the dominant near-term risk event. Despite positive geopolitical news (Iran deal, falling oil), the combination of expensive valuations, persistent inflation, and a live Fed meeting warrants a cautious posture. We demand a larger margin of safety before any new buy.
